response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman with broad web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware expertise that is entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, deep-learning workload performance analysis, or hardware-software co-design at the silicon level. The low-latency DLang microservices and C/C++ measurement-IDE work are the closest adjacencies, but they do not approach the SoC modeling and accelerator architecture demanded here. Education is a BS only, and the role's heavy hardware/ML-systems focus makes this a fundamental domain mismatch.
strengths: Strong low-latency/high-performance software background (async I/O, fibers, caching, millions of req/s)
Some embedded and C/C++ systems experience (LabWindows/CVI, robotics)
Broad, deep engineering versatility with strong testing and CI/CD discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or AI accelerator/GPU architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or workload analysis background
Lacks the hardware-software co-design and system architecture depth the role centers on

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP and accelerator hardware expertise
